mnemonics.title: I Prefer Zesty Tarts (IPZT): Independent, Populati
mnemonics.content: μ) / (σ/√n), comparing a sample mean to a population mean, assuming independence.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: A sampling distribution describes the distribution of a statistic (like the sample mean, x̄) over many samples from the same population.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: The Central Limit Theorem states that the sampling distribution of the mean will approach a normal distribution as the sample size increases, regardless of the population's shape.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: The standard error (e.g., σ/√n for the mean) measures the variability of a sampling distribution and decreases as sample size increases.
